questions.question: Which CSS property specifies the number of columns content should be divided into?
questions.explanation: The column-count property sets the exact number of columns for multi-column layout. The browser divides the content evenly across that many columns.

questions.question: What does the column-gap property control?
choices: The space between the column content and the container edge
The space between column rules and column content
The vertical spacing between rows of columns
questions.explanation: The column-gap property sets the size of the gap between adjacent columns in a multi-column layout. The default value is normal, which is typically 1em.

questions.question: Which property draws a line between columns, similar to a border?
questions.explanation: The column-rule property is a shorthand for column-rule-width, column-rule-style, and column-rule-color. It draws a line in the gap between columns.

questions.question: What does column-span: all do?
choices: Makes all columns the same width
Makes an element span across all columns
Distributes content evenly across all columns
Applies the rule to all columns simultaneously
questions.explanation: Setting column-span: all on an element inside a multi-column container makes that element span across all columns, breaking the column flow. This is useful for headings.

questions.question: The ___ property specifies the ideal minimum width for each column, letting the browser determine the column count.
questions.explanation: The column-width property sets a suggested optimal width for columns. The browser creates as many columns of at least that width as will fit in the container.

questions.question: The ___ shorthand property sets both column-width and column-count in a single declaration.
questions.explanation: The columns shorthand accepts a column-width and/or column-count value. For example, columns: 200px 3 sets a preferred width of 200px with a maximum of 3 columns.
